2000-07-01.title: Leading Candidates in Mexico As Close on Issues as in Polls
2000-07-01.abstract: Leading candidates in Mexican presidential election are seen close on major issues; Francsico Labastida Ochoa of governing party and Vicente Fox Quesada of right-of-center opposition, who have been running neck and neck, are so close on economic issues that they use same slogan: 'An economy with a human face'; both candidates play down nationalism that used to be a campaign mainstay and favor good relations with United States (M)

2000-07-01.title: Killings Drive Indonesians Into Risky Boats and Dangerous Seas
2000-07-01.abstract: Fighting in Maluku islands, Indonesia, between Muslims and Christians spurs many islanders to flee, driving them into risky boats and dangerous seas; nearly 500 people are feared dead following sinking of their battered ferry; passengers were desperately fleeing Moslem gangs that were killing people, apparently in revenge for what they describe as wave of killings by Christians in December; photo; map (L)

2000-07-01.title: Korean Summit:Bad for Stocks?
2000-07-01.abstract: AN ARTICLE in the June 24-25 Money Report concerned the investment prospects of bonds issued by pariah states, focusing in particular on North Korean debt and the impact that the recent Korean summit might have on it. Barton Biggs, the global strategist at Morgan Stanley Dean Witter & Co., has been considering another potential impact of the summit meeting on South Korean stocks.

2000-07-01.title: Alfred C. Ulmer Jr., 83, Officer In U.S. Intelligence Agencies
2000-07-01.abstract: Alfred C Ulmer Jr, officer in US intelligence agencies from World War II until 1962, dies at age 83 (S)

2000-07-01.title: Iceland, a Natural Genetics Lab
2000-07-01.abstract: GENOMICS is frequently compared to the computer industry of the 1980s, but there is at least one big difference: the action is not concentrated in California. Some of the most interesting research is taking place far off the beaten track.

2000-07-02.title: Terry Forrestal, 52, a Stuntman In Bond Films and 'Braveheart'
2000-07-02.abstract: Terry Forrestal, British stuntman who staged and performed action sequences for such Hollywood films as Braveheart and Titanic, died on June 10 in jumping accident in Norway; he was 52 years old; photo (M)

2000-07-02.title: U.S. Helps Colombian Leader, But His Woes Pile Up at Home
2000-07-02.abstract: Pres Andres Pastrana, who took office in 1998 with highest vote total of any candidate in Colombia's history, finds his standing sinking, even though he has just received vote of confidence from US in form of $1.3 billion in emergency military aid (M)

2000-07-02.title: Castro Says He Will Press Effort to Change U.S. Policy
2000-07-02.abstract: Fidel Castro vows to press campaign to change American policy on Cuba, but suggests he does expect immediate success with either of candidates to succeed Pres Clinton; message is read at first rally since return of six-year-old Elian Gonzalez, subject of custody battle between father and Miami relatives; photo of rally protesting US embargo; map (M)

2000-07-02.title: 10 JEWS CONVICTED BY IRANIAN COURT IN ESPIONAGE CASE
2000-07-02.abstract: Iranian judge convicts 10 of 13 Jewish men accused by Revolutionary Court of spying for Israel and sentences them to prison terms ranging from 4 to 13 years; two Muslim men convicted of aiding spy ring are sentenced to two and four years; two other Muslims and three Jews are acquitted; Pres Clinton says he is deeply disturbed at trial that did not give defendants due process of law; close-knit Jewish community in Shiraz, where many defendants were among religious leaders, is grim, viewing ruling as warning; photo; appeal will cite lack of any evidence other than confessions made after defendants had been jailed for nearly 16 months without counsel; convictions may have tangible effect on Pres Mohammad Khatami's bid for warmer relations with West (M)

2000-07-02.title: Israeli Incursions Add to Friction Over Border
2000-07-02.abstract: Quick deployment of UN peackeepers and Lebanese forces in stretch of southern Lebanon abandoned by Israel has been thrown into doubt by apparent Israeli inscursions across 1923 border; map (S)

2000-07-03.title: With Time Short, Albright Stays Aloft
2000-07-03.abstract: Sec Madeleine K Albright, who has visited 82 nations since taking office, wants to stress her vision of democracy in last six months of her term; says her travels show how job has changed since end of cold war and how foreign policy issues have expanded; photo (M)

2000-07-03.title: In Canada, Gay Pride Can Be Part of Scouts' Honor
2000-07-03.abstract: Canada has become one of the world's most tolerant countries regarding homosexuality; Scouts Canada has inaugurated its first official gay and lesbian scout troop; Canada has also just adopted omnibus law that erases virtually all legal distinctions between heterosexual marriages and same sex unions; photo (M)

2000-07-03.title: A Race to Save Roman Splendors From Drowning
2000-07-03.abstract: International team of archaeologists is racing to unearth and save mosaics and artifacts in Zeugma, Turkey, site of ancient Roman town, which will soon be inundated by lake forming behind new Euphrates River dam; some Turkish archaeologists and government officials seem uncomfortable with widespread publicity abroad, apparently viewing foreign clamor as interference that borders on insult; plans for at least 10 more dams in region in coming decades could spur growing conflict between advocates of economic development and of historical preservation; photo (M)

2000-07-03.title: Ugandans Vote to Continue One-Party System
2000-07-03.abstract: Final returns show Ugandan voters overwhelmingly endorsed Pres Yoweri Museveni's 'no-party' political system; Museveni and Pres Paul Kagame of Rwanda, nominal allies in fighting in Congo, meet to defuse tensions in wake of June skirmishes between their troops in Kisangani, Congo; fighting in Congo is unpopular in Uganda, and Museveni's opponents raised issue in pressing for return to traditional political parties; opponents hold low voter turnout reflects success of their boycott and widespread discontent with Museveni (M)

2000-07-03.title: An Anxious Vigil in Mexico's Political Trenches
2000-07-03.abstract: Supporters of Vicente Fox Quesada in Mexico's presidential campaign carefully monitor voting in places like Orizaba to guard against fraud by ruling party, which backs Francisco Labastida Ochoa; Fox's campaign, boosted by palpable desire for change among peasants who have long backed ruling party, watches carefully over newest and most vulnerable supporters; map; photo (M)

2000-07-04.title: Strong Feelings of Pride Over Orderly Balloting
2000-07-04.abstract: Mexicans of various backgrounds, professions and political stripes seem stunned and excited by Vicente Fox Quesada's decisive triumph over one of longest ruling political machines in world; Mexicans express pride in peaceful way votes were cast and counted, in way they overcame their fear of change and embraced it, and in feeling that their country will be more widely viewed as sophisticated world player now; photo (M)

2000-07-04.title: MEXICO, VOTING IN NEW LEADER, BEGINS A POLITICAL SEA CHANGE
2000-07-04.abstract: Pres-elect Vicente Fox Quesada of Mexico begins transition from republic led for decades by autocratic presidents to one in which political power is now up for grabs; election returns show he won 43 percent of votes, while Francisco Labastida Ochoa, candidate of governing party, won 36 percent and Cuauhtemoc Cardenas Solorzano, leftist candidate, got 17 percent; Fox's victory over governing party is far more decisive than any poll had predicted; Fox receives contratulatory phone calls from world leaders, including Pres Clinton, and meets with Pres Ernesto Zedillo; Fox says he assured Zedillo that he will not carry out purge of government bureaucracy after he takes office Dec 1; says two will meet frequently before than to plan next year's budget and to discuss transition; Fox will preside over government unused to coalition politics; photos (M)

2000-07-04.title: Salvadorans Balk at American Plan to Use Airport
2000-07-04.abstract: United States request to set up military logistics point in El Salvador for its war on drugs touches nerve in that country; American advisers, intelligence and money not long ago helped fuel devastating civil war there; encroaching role of United States is seen by some as infringing on national sovereignty; legislators from FMLN, who form largest single bloc in Assembly, say accord turns over to US monitoring and enforcement tasks that rightly belong to El Salvador's own police and military; they bristle at general references that allow American personnel access to any government institutions needed to carry out their mission; for El Salvador, larger issue is what role their own military should play in addressing internal security after dozen years in which it took part in war that left 60,000 Salvadorans dead; photo; map (M)

2000-07-04.title: Chechen Rebels Kill 37 Russian Soldiers In Truck Bombings
2000-07-04.abstract: Chechen suicide bombers set off series of blasts throughout breakaway republic, killing at least 37 Russian soldiers and wounding 74; five trucks laden with explosives blow up in towns and cities ostensibly under Russian control; carefully coordinated rebel attack occurs just a week after Russian military declared that war is all but won; as toll of dead and wounded mounts, Moscow vows to break back of rebel resistance; rebels respond by saying they plan still more bombings; rumors are rife that Grozny, Chechen capital, will be attacked next; photo; map (M)

2000-07-04.title: A Threat By Austria On Sanctions
2000-07-04.abstract: Chancellor Wolfgang Schussel of Austria says his country will block expansion of European Union until it lifts sanctions imposed last February to punish country for letting party of Jorg Haider into government; sanctions have had no serious economic effect on Austria, but they rankle with Austrians; approval of any of dozen countries seeking admission to European Union must be unanimous, so Austria could play spoiler if it does not get its way (M)

2000-07-05.title: Moscow Blames Poor Security for Bombing Deaths in Chechnya
2000-07-05.abstract: Nikolai Patrushev, chief of Russia's intelligence service, blames negligence by Russian commanders for suicide bombings by Chechen rebels that killed more than 30 soldiers; says that Federal Security Service alerted military that attacks were being planned but that someone failed to take proper precautions (M)

2000-07-05.title: France's Boom Sends Some Bright Ideas Into Summer 2001 : Optimistic Color Sweeps Paris
2000-07-05.abstract: PARIS—  Can life really be this good — bright sunshine colors even for work? A Palm Pilot encased in a Day-Glo purse? Hawaiian print shirts that bring vacation wear to main street? Even pastel shoes?

2000-07-05.title: Mexico's Defeated Leadership Squabbles Over the Next Step
2000-07-05.abstract: Battle erupts within Mexico's governing Institutional Revolutionary Party following its election defeat, with several rival leaders maneuvering for control during painful transition that party leaders say will test its powers of survival; near chaos grips party after leader, Dulce Maria Sauri, resigns with expectation that entire cabinet will leave with her (M)

2000-07-05.title: Misery Index Of U.N. Panel Finds Africa Is Worst Off
2000-07-05.abstract: United Nations' annual Human Development Report states that Africans south of Sahara, convulsed by wars and weakened by natural disasters, suffer broadest range of social and economic disadvantages (S)

2000-07-05.title: VICTOR IN MEXICO PLANS TO OVERHAUL LAW ENFORCEMENT
2000-07-05.abstract: Mexico's president-elect, Vicente Fox Quesada, pledges to rebuild country's federal law enforcement system and wipe out corruption that has crippled nation for decades; is likely to use executive powers to create a Ministry of Security and Justice, similar to Federal Bureal of Investigation, that would be responsible for all federal police, significantly reducing powers of Interior Ministry and attorney general; says he will meet with Pres Clinton and both major presidential candidates to press proposal to open US-Mexico border to less restricted flow of immigrants; expounds on measures his new government will take to deal with range of Mexico's most persistent and complex problems, including fight against poverty, efforts to stem flow of drugs and unresolved conflict in Chiapas; photo (M)

2000-07-06.title: Ugandan Villagers Avoiding Cult Massacre Site
2000-07-06.abstract: Residents of Kanungu, Uganda, are avoiding compound where hundreds of members of religious sect, Movement for the Restoration of the Ten Commandments of God, were murdered in March; circumstances surrounding deaths still remain mystery; death toll put at 780; map; photo (M)

2000-07-06.title: FUNGUS CONSIDERED AS A TOOL TO KILL COCA IN COLOMBIA
2000-07-06.abstract: Colombia, under pressure from US, reluctantly agrees to take first step toward developing powerful biological herbicide against its coca and heroin-poppy fields; US has been quietly debating for years ways of conducting field tests of such a herbicide, developed from fungus that occurs naturally in many types of cocoa and other plants; Colombian officials say they are completing proposal to UN that will include testing for presence of fungus, Fusarium oxysporum, in coca, raw material of cocaine; government is suporting project as Pres Clinton is about to sign bill providing $1.3 billion in aid to Colombia to fight drug traffickers and insurgents who protect their trade (M)

2000-07-06.title: Pentagon Gives a Preview of Missile Defense Test Tomorrow
2000-07-06.abstract: Pentagon gives preview of test of missile defense system, scheduled for July 6, in Roslyn, Va, office of Boeing Co, prime contractor for program; exercise, third and most demanding in series of 19 tests for national missile defense system, will last for 30 minutes (M)

2000-07-06.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-07-06.abstract: EUROPE    FRANCE: U.S. ESPIONAGE INQUIRY  The European Parliament in Strasbourg has voted to form a committee to investigate allegations that Washington's vast surveillance system, called Echelon, is being used to conduct industrial espionage now that the cold war is over. The system, maintained by the United States and several allies including Britain, listens to and watches millions of telephone, fax and e-mail messages each day. The committee is expected to report back in about eight months.    Suzanne Daley (NYT)

2000-07-06.title: Familiar Foe for Mexico's New Leader: Corruption
2000-07-06.abstract: Focus on plans by Mexican president-elect Vicente Fox Quesada to fight crime and corruption by overhauling country's federal law enforcement institutions; experts say plans, which would require several constitutional amendments, suggest that he intends to bring power of presidency to bear in crackdown more than his recent predecessors; photo (M)

2000-07-07.title: Change in Yugoslav Constitution Allows Milosevic to Seek Another Term as President
2000-07-07.abstract: Yugoslav Parliament approves constitutional changes that allow Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic to run for a new term and that diminish influence of Montenegro, Serbia's small sister republic; changes require direct popular election of the Yugoslav president and upper house of Parliament, allow two four-year terms for president and raise threshold for impeachment; effect will give Milosevic chance of remaining in power past July of next year, when his current term expires; until now, Yugoslav president was elected by Parliament and could serve only one four-year term; upper house of Parliament was itself elected by Parliaments of the two republics, Serbia and Montenegro; changes make Milosevic's plans for elections less dependent on Montenegro's consent and may hasten Montenegro's effort to break away (M)

2000-07-07.title: Palestinian Investment Fund, No Longer Secret, Will Close
2000-07-07.abstract: Palestinian Authority divulges details of its long-hidden investment porrtfolio; chief economic adviser Mohammed Rachid says that he will preside over dissolution of the financial empire he amassed at Yasir Arafat's request, pledging that holdings will either be divested or transferred to a new, publicly accountable Palestinian Investment Fund by year's end; according to audited statement sent to International Monetary Fund and published on authority's Web site, the holding company now boasts assets of $345 million, including $292 million in equity stakes in three dozen companies (M)

2000-07-07.title: Critics Asking Clinton to Stop Advancing Missile Plan
2000-07-07.abstract: Dr Theodore A Postol, a leading critic of national missile defense, says in letter to Pres Clinton that Clinton's advisers have made misleading statements about proposed missile shield and urges creating an independent commission of top scientists to evaluate plan; American Physical Society, Federation of American Scientists and Union of Concerned Scientists jointly announce that they have urged Clinton not to deploy missile defense system, regardless of how its ground-based interceptor performs in coming test; Greenpeace is sending a Dutch icebreaker, Arctic Sunrise, to designated 'hazard zone' near launching site at Vandenberg Air Force Base in California to try to stop test; Defense Department issues statement defending integrity of program (M)

2000-07-07.title: Oldest Son's Revels Embarrass Blair
2000-07-07.abstract: Prime Min Tony Blair of Britain, who has made a point of keeping his four young children out of news, is embarrassed by front-page headlines reporting arrest of his 16-year-old son, Euan, for public drunkenness (S)

2000-07-07.title: Congressional Report Says Corruption Is Stifling Bosnia
2000-07-07.abstract: General Accounting Office says that pervasive crime and corruption are blocking success of Dayton peace accord in Bosnia and that Clinton administration should consider suspending its extensive aid until local authorities show a determination to fight such abuses; says corruption could interfere with eventual withdrawal of United States and other NATO troops (M)

2000-07-08.title: REPORT DIMS HOPE FOR AIDS THERAPY TO PROTECT BABIES
2000-07-08.abstract: United Nations-sponsored study finds that while a simple drug regimen can stop many mothers with HIV from infecting their children during pregnancy and childbirth, the protection does not last for as long as experts had hoped and that the therapy appears to leave children vulnerable to infection from breast milk; finding safe alternatives to breast-feeding is daunting challenge in Africa, where overwhelming majority of women breast-feed their infants for long periods; findings are certain to renew often bitter controversy over breast-feeding (M)

2000-07-08.title: ANTIMISSILE TEST FAILS OVER PACIFIC, PENTAGON REPORTS
2000-07-08.abstract: Clinton administration's hopes for moving forward with national missile defense system are dealt setback when missile fired from Marshall Islands in Pacific fails to hit a mock warhead launched 4,300 miles away in California; test failure is certain to reinforce calls in Congress that decision on whether to move forward with a national missile defense program should be left next president (M)

2000-07-08.title: China Moves To Cut Power Of Microsoft
2000-07-08.abstract: Chinese government backs Linux operating system in effort to prevent country from becoming overly dependent on Microsoft Corp's Windows operating system; some Chinese warn that secret holes in Microsoft's computer code might allow US government access to Chinese networks or even enable it, in time of war, to shut those networks down; Microsoft says such fears are nonsense; Linux source code, unlike Microsoft's, is open for all to see and is freely distributed, and this touch of communist ideal appeals to China's Marxist leaders (M)

2000-07-08.title: Just a Little Garlic Overpowers Asian Trade Ties
2000-07-08.abstract: SEOUL—  A little bit of garlic goes a long way toward spicing up Korea's national dish, kimchi. But lately a little bit of Chinese garlic has injected a foul odor into trade relations between South Korea and Beijing.

2000-07-08.title: Cardenas Journal; The Town That's Proud to Say Elian Lives Here
2000-07-08.abstract: People of Cardenas, Cuba, the home of Elian Gonzalez, begin to reclaim their daily routines now that the boy is back in Cuba; photo; map (M)

2000-07-09.title: MEDICINE MERCHANTS: Patents and Patients; As Devastating Epidemics Increase, Nations Take On Drug Companies
2000-07-09.abstract: Special report, Medicine Merchants, examines titanic struggle between pharmaceutical companies and public health advocates over price of drugs and right to produce and market them in third world countries as epidemics increase worldwide; situation in Ongata Rongai, Kenya, where critically ill are unable to obtain drugs because of high costs discussed; maps; photos (M)

2000-07-09.title: KEY MISSILE PARTS ARE LEFT UNTESTED AS BOOSTER FAILS
2000-07-09.abstract: Crucial test in Pentagon's complex, $60 billion antimissile defensive system fails after high-speed interceptor 'kill vehicle' designed to destroy dummy warhead fails to separate from booster rocket; failure will make it politically and diplomatically more difficult for Pres Clinton to move ahead with plan administration contends is crucial to defend against attack, but which opponents say is technically unsound, militarily unnecessary and waste of money; reinforces calls in Congress to leave decision to next president; failure was in well-developed technology, used successfully in rockets that have launched satellites and missiles for decades; photo (M)

2000-07-09.title: Putin Describes an Ill Russia and Prescribes Strong Democracy
2000-07-09.abstract: Russian Pres Vladimir V Putin presents forbidding state-of-Russia address to Parliament and national television audience; says Russians have built only 'carcass' of civil society, and have yet to shoulder basic obligations, such as obeying laws; warns falling birthrate and aging population threaten to make country 'senile' nation; says economy is actually house of cards supported by high oil prices; warns growing gap between advanced nations and Russia is pushing country into third world; says not much can be remedied until Parliament ratifies his plans to concentrate power in Kremlin and destroy barnacled system of regulations, hidden favors and political empire-building that is strangling economy (M)

2000-07-09.title: Two Sides Share Subdued View of Mideast Summit Talks
2000-07-09.abstract: Even though residents of Israel, West Bank and Gaza are prisoners of dreary pursuit for peace, only Jewish settlers are treating Camp David summit as pivotal event; only in sttlements has daily life been altered to plan for what many see as most significant battle of political lives; photo (M)

2000-07-09.title: Strategy Misfire: Missile Failure Carries Political Cost
2000-07-09.abstract: News analysis: Clinton administration has reaped all of diplomatic disadvantages of program to build national missile defense and none of potential rewards with recent failure of Pentagon's antimissile test (M)

2000-07-10.title: BARAK'S COALITION CRUMBLES ON EVE OF SUMMIT TALKS
2000-07-10.abstract: Israeli government of Prime Min Ehud Barak crumbles as three rightist parties quit in protest against anticipated concessions to Palestinians, leaving him on uncertain ground on eve of departure for Camp David summit with Yasir Arafat and Pres Clinton; loses parliamentary majority and broad coalition, constructed precisely to build stable foundation of support for difficult peace moves; three parties reject Barak's pleas for unity, defecting to previously weak opposition and upsetting political balance of power; Barak addresses nation; defiantly says coalition will not affect trip to summit, which he maintains is backed by Israelis who elected him by overwhelming majority on peacemaking platform; says as lifelong soldier and fledgling politician he has direct bond with voters; possible effects of government's collapse on three-way retreat-style summit discussed; photos (M)

2000-07-10.title: Pope Declares His 'Bitterness' Over Gay Event
2000-07-10.abstract: Pope John Paul II sxpresses 'bitterness' over gay pride festival held in Rome during Roman Catholic Holy Year; says event is affront to church and Christian values of Italian capital (S)

2000-07-10.title: Decision on Defense System May Fall to Next President
2000-07-10.abstract: Senior advisers to Pres Clinton say they still expect him to make decision before end of summer on whether to proceed with missile defense system, while acknowledging that recent failure of major test makes it far more likely that his successor will ultimately decide fate of project; comments by national security adviser Samuel R Berger and others noted (M)

2000-07-10.title: 7 Killed in 2 Bombings in Southern Russia, Not Far From Chechnya
2000-07-10.abstract: Two bombings in southern Russian provinces close to Chechnya kill seven people, week after Chechen rebels killed 33 Russian soldiers in wave of truck-bomb attacks (S)

2000-07-10.title: U.N.'s Plan for Kosovo Threatens to Unravel
2000-07-10.abstract: First agreement between Serbs and UN administration in Gracanica, Serbia, which may provide better protection for Serbian minority, threatens to unravel key Albanian participation in international efforts to build democratic, tolerant Kosovo; year into effort to rebuild new Kosovo, many in UN administration express weariness over recalcitrance of Albanian leaders who are jostling for power and seem willing to fight out differences on streets; map; photo (M)

2000-07-11.title: South Africa Faults Critics of Its President on AIDS Stance
2000-07-11.abstract: South African government, striking defiant and combative tone, lashes out at critics at 13th international conference on AIDS in Durban who expressed disappointment with Pres Thabo Mbeki for opening conference by describing extreme poverty, rather than AIDS, as biggest killer in Africa (M)

2000-07-11.title: French Killer In Pivotal Case To Stay in Jail
2000-07-11.abstract: Patrick Henry, whose trial in 1977 for killing seven-year-old boy helped abolish death penalty in France, is refused parole (S)

2000-07-11.title: Report Challenges the Policymaking Elite : French Diplomats Told To Go Back to School
2000-07-11.abstract: PARIS—  France has decided to send its governing elite, including its redoubtable diplomats, back to school to learn how to cope with new challenges in a more competitive, globalized world.

2000-07-11.title: San Salvador Journal; Pilgrims Take Inspiration From Martyrs of the 80's
2000-07-11.abstract: Tens of thousands of pilgrims from around world have trekked to San Salvador, El Salvador, since end of civil conflict to seek out Jesuit residence, where soldiers killed six priests and their housekeeper and daughter in 1989, as well as chapel of local hospital, where Archbishop Oscar Arnulfo Romero, was shot dead during Mass in 1980; have transformed both places into local shrines; others also visit place in countryside where four American churchwomen were killed by soldiers in December 1980; maps; photo (M)

2000-07-11.title: Moment of Truth on Tough Issues
2000-07-11.abstract: Major issues facing Israeli and Palestinian negotiators at Camp David peace summit discussed; map (M)

2000-07-12.title: Intermittent Drug Therapy for AIDS Is Tested
2000-07-12.abstract: International AIDS conference in Durban, South Africa, hears promising report from Dr Scott Holmberg of Centers for Disease Control and Prevention on trials aimed at giving long breaks in treatment to people infected with HIV; aim is to help body cope with virus on its own for varying periods and make it easier for infected people to take the drugs; Dr Mauro Schechter of Brazil says drugs other than protease inhibitors deserve some of the credit they regularly get for lowering death rates from AIDS; Dr Anthony S Fauci of National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases says that eradicating HIV is not possible with drugs currently available and that prolonged courses of drug combinations are not feasible for most infected people (M)

2000-07-12.title: Arab-Israeli Talks Open at Camp David
2000-07-12.abstract: Prime Min Ehud Barak, Yasir Arafat and Pres Clinton begin their summit meeting at Camp David in Maryland, and Clinton speaks in idealistic tones of securing a just and enduring end to Israeli-Palestinian conflict; Israeli officials say they will soon resolve their dispute with Clinton administration over contract to sell Phalcon advanced radar system to the Chinese; photos (M)

2000-07-12.title: World Briefing
2000-07-12.abstract: MIDDLE EAST    IRAN: ACQUITTED IN STUDENT ATTACK  A military court acquitted a former Tehran police chief of ordering an attack on a student dormitory that led to a week of pro-reform protests. The verdict brought immediate criticism from students. Farhad Nazari and 17 subordinates were charged with injuring scores of students and killing one in the attack last July. Two defendants were found guilty. ''Such rulings will lead to social insecurity and people will lose their confidence in the judiciary,'' said Ibrahim Sheik, a student leader, a sentiment echoed by other students.   Nazila Fathi (NYT)    IRAN: KHATAMI PLEA TO U.S.  President Mohammad Khatami, on a visit to Germany, urged the United States to take the next step toward reviving diplomatic relations with Tehran. ''You can be sure that when the United States has the key in its hand, it will not give it away,'' he said on German public television. He said he recognized a ''new turn'' in Washington, but that the shift did not go far enough.   Victor Homola (NYT)

2000-07-12.title: Mexican Murder Suspect Is Sought by U.S.
2000-07-12.abstract: US seeks extradition from Mexico of Agustin Vasquez Mendoza, who is suspected of the 1994 murder of Richard Fass, an American anti-drug agent (S)

2000-07-12.title: Russian Media Conglomerate Searched Again by Authorities
2000-07-12.abstract: Russian prosecutors search offices of Media-Most, media conglomerate owned by Vladimir A Gusinsky, for the second time in two months; skeptics suspect that Kremlin is trying to silence one of its few prominent critics (M)

2000-07-13.title: U.S. AND VIETNAM ARE SAID TO AGREE ON NORMAL TRADE
2000-07-13.abstract: United States and Vietnamese officials meeting in Washington reportedly complete sweeping trade agreement that would allow generally unfettered commerce between the two nations for first time since end of Vietnam War; agreement, which comes after four years of fitful negotiations, is expected to be signed within a week; but Congress will still have to approve agreement and extend normal trade privileges to Vietnam, a vote that some Clinton administration officials believe is unlikely this election year (M)

2000-07-13.title: Mexico's Ousted Party Tries to Regroup After Stunning Defeat
2000-07-13.abstract: Elder statesmen of Mexico's Institutional Revolutionary Party agree on formula aimed at maintaining internal discipline as party's long monopoly over Mexican power and wealth draws to a close; 21 state governors, after meeting with Pres Ernesto Zedillo, announce that they have convinced party's top leader, Dulce Maria Sauri, to remain in her post for a few months while party decides how to choose her replacement (M)

2000-07-13.title: Travel Boom Pulls Japan and South Korea Closer
2000-07-13.abstract: Warming relations between South Korea and Japan after years of mistrust and disdain are reflected by explosion in tourism between the two countries; Fukuoka, which is close to Korean peninsula, has become favored jumping-off point for Japanese travelers; map; photos (M)

2000-07-13.title: Russian Auto Executive Faces Tax Charges
2000-07-13.abstract: Russian authorities bring tax evasion charges against top executives of AvtoVAZ, country's biggest carmaker, as part of Pres Vladimir V Putin's efforts to loosen grip of Russia's richest men on country's most profitable enterprises; charges against executives may be a move against Boris A Berezovsky, influential member of Parliament and friend of former Pres Boris N Yeltsin's family; Berezovsky holds major stake in AvtoVAZ (M)

2000-07-13.title: Engineer Charging Antimissile Fraud Is Snared in a Federal Clash
2000-07-13.abstract: Nira Schwartz, senior engineer who leveled charges of fraud in antimissile research against military contractor TRW, is becoming caught in clash between Defense Department, which is trying to question her about leaks of military secrets, and Congressional and criminal investigators, who are gearing up to examine merits of her accusations that TRW falsified work that was meant to help antimissile interceptors distinguish incoming warheads from decoys; Schwartz says Pentagon plans to have security investigators question her are clearly aimed at shielding $60 billion antimisile plan from scrutiny (M)

2000-07-14.title: Barak and Arafat Hold Impromptu Meeting
2000-07-14.abstract: Unknown to their American hosts, Prime Min Ehud Barak of Israel and Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat held private, impromptu meeting at Arafat's cabin on night of July 12; substance of what was said is not described in brief account given by State Department, if indeed it is known, but fact that such encounter took place is taken as sign that serious effort is being made by both sides (M)

2000-07-14.title: World Briefing
2000-07-14.abstract: THE AMERICAS    CANADA: NEW INDIAN CHIEF -- Canadian Indian tribes have elected as their national chief a Quebec Cree known for leading a North American protest campaign that stopped a major hydroelectric dam planned for northern Quebec. Matthew Coon Come, a former Cree chief, told delegates of the Assembly of First Nations that his main priority will be securing self-government and land rights.     James Brooke (NYT)

2000-07-14.title: Vietnam and the U.S. Sign Pact Aimed at Promoting Trade
2000-07-14.abstract: United States and Vietnam sign lengthy trade agreement that, if put into effect fully, would transform America's onetime enemy from quasi-socialist reboubt into Asia's newest capitalist recruit; Pres Clinton hails agreement as 'historic event'; Vietnam, with its small, troubled and highly protected economy is risking far more than United States; but agreement requires US to reduce its tariffs substantially on Vietnamese imports, concession that has often proves a tough sell in Congress; it is unlikely vote will come on agreement during Pres Clinton's term (M)

2000-07-14.title: Colombians Tell of Massacre, as Army Stood By
2000-07-14.abstract: Massacre in tiny village of El Salado highlights United States and human rights groups' reservations about Colombia's military leadership, its human rights record and its collaboration with paramilitary units; more then 300 armed men marched into village in February, and with list of names began summoning residents for judgment; troops ordered liquor and music, then embarked on calculated three-day rampage of torture, rape and killing of scores of people they accused of collaborating with left-wing guerrillas who have long been presence in area; military and police units just few miles away not only made no effort to stop slaughter, but also set up roadblock, preventing human rights groups from getting to village; while military has opened three investigations into massacre, top officials insist that fighting was under way in village between guerrillas and paramilitary forces, not a series of executions; they also insist that colonel in charge of region, Rodrigo Quinones Cardenas, who has since been promoted to general, has been persecuted by government and human rights groups; map; photos (L)

2000-07-14.title: Leatherhead Journal; The White Van Man of England: Oh, He's a Devil!
2000-07-14.abstract: Britain has most congested roads in Europe, and probably more road rage than any other country; much of this hostility is focused on so-called 'white van men,' drivers of country's estimated 2.5 million, traditionally white, commercial vans, who are viewed as aggressive, no-holds-barred kings of the road; several van drivers in town of Leatherland comment on their driving habits and criticism directed at them; map; photos (Leatherland Journal) (M)

2000-07-15.title: Russian Puzzle: What Does War on Tycoons Mean?
2000-07-15.abstract: Russian government campaign against tycoons who run five of nation's most powerful business combines has puzzling political implications, for it is hard to tell, yet, whether it is the payoff on Pres Vladimir Putin's pledge to apply the same laws to both the tycoon and kiosk operator or the beginning of some darker and more subtle bloodletting (M)

2000-07-15.title: European Union Is Moving to Lift Sanctions It Imposed on Austria
2000-07-15.abstract: European Union is moving to lift the sanctions imposed on Austria after Jorg Haider's far-right party joined the government; France, the leader in imposing sanctions, has just taken over rotating presidency of European Union, and both its presige and, perhaps, the timely expansion of the union are riding on its being able to deliver on a difficult agenda; Austrian officials have threatened to use their veto to block any sort of progress (M)

2000-07-15.title: Radical Tax Plan, a Spur for 'Germany Inc.,' Is Approved
2000-07-15.abstract: German Parliament passes radical tax program that will slash rates and allow corporations to sell subsidiaries or shares in other companies without paying any tax on their profit; change is expected to revolutionize structure of German business because it clears the way for the big banks and insurance companies to sell more than $100 billion in shares in industrial companies like DaimlerChrysler and Siemens; vote is unexpected victory for Chancellor Gerhard Schroder, a Social Democrat; his tax plan has made him the hero of corporate elite; photo (M)

2000-07-15.title: Camp David Talks in a Lull Before an Expected Whirl of Decision-Making
2000-07-15.abstract: Israeli and Palestinian leaders spend their fourth day in secluded negotiations at Camp David, but serious decision-making has yet to begin (M)

2000-07-15.title: World Briefing
2000-07-15.abstract: AFRICA    ZIMBABWE: LAND REDISTRIBUTION -- President Robert Mugabe said his government was to start redistributing land today to war veterans who invaded hundreds of white-owned farms earlier this year in support of his land seizure program. Speaking at the burial of an independence war commander, he said he would not brook domestic opposition or accept foreign criticism. Last month, the government listed 804 farms for seizure for its black resettlement program.   (Reuters)

2000-07-16.title: Haiti Refuses To Back Down On Elections
2000-07-16.abstract: Haitian officials say they will stand by results of their recent elections despite threats from international donors to suspend aid because of vote-counting method they deem unfair and unacceptable (M)

2000-07-16.title: Canada Sees Violent Turn In the Fight On Abortion
2000-07-16.abstract: Canada, traditionally North America's liberal bastion in abortion war, is suddenly gripped by polarizing debate that has gone beyond ideology to an array of violent attacks and threats; in space of one week, right-wing politician with strong anti-abortion views is elected of Canada's main opposition party, Vancouver gynecologist who performs abortions is stabbed in back and death threat is telephoned to leader of national trials of abortion drug (M)

2000-07-16.title: John Morgan, Etiquette Maven For the British, Is Dead at 41
2000-07-16.abstract: John Morgan, British etiquette expert, dies at age 41; photo (M)

2000-07-16.title: Africa's AIDS Crisis: Finding Common Ground
2000-07-16.abstract: Lawrence K Altman news analysis of Africa's AIDS crisis, as 13th annual International Aids conference ends; finds that what is implicit in remarks of both South Africa Pres Thabo Mbeki, who emphasizes social factors like poverty as major force behind AIDS, and former Pres Nelson Mandela's forceful call for action to stop AIDS epidemic, is idea that some AIDS problems can be attacked now, even without vaccines and cures that have yet to emerge from laboratories in developed world (L)

2000-07-16.title: Italy Journal; On a '44 Battlefield, a Salute for a Black Hero
2000-07-16.abstract: Black American veterans who fought in Italy in World War II return to village of Sommocolonia to honor their comrade, Lt John Fox, who was killed in battle on Dec 26, 1944; Fox, who ordered his own men to fire on his position to stem enemy attack, was awarded Medal of Honor more than 50 years later; anguish that black veterans relive is echoed among Italian war survivors who welcome their return; just as black comrades and their relatives cannot shed their bitterness over United States' long refusal to recognize combat records of black servicemen fully, many Italian veterans cannot forgive their own countrymen who fought against them more than 50 years ago; photos; map (L)

2000-07-17.title: Tamil Rebels Said to Recruit Child Soldiers
2000-07-17.abstract: University Teachers for Human Rights, a Tamil rights organization, charges that Tamil rebels preparing for showdown with Sri Lankan Army for control of Jaffna, Sri Lanka's most important Tamil town, have been invading schoolrooms and rounding up children in early teens to send into battle; describes reign of terror in region (M)

2000-07-17.title: Clinton Voices Some Optimism On Peace Talks
2000-07-17.abstract: Pres Clinton breaks strict news blackout surrounding Camp David summit between Prime Min Ehud Barak of Israel and Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat to tell New York Daily News that he is more optimistic than when talks began six days ago, but is not sure agreement will be reached; says talks are difficult and are unlike anything else he has ever dealt with; says what troubles him most is prospect of what could happen if they fail; comments while rebutting allegations in new book that his wife, Hillary Rodham Clinton, made anti-Semitic remarks about one of his campaign managers nearly three decades ago (M)

2000-07-17.title: Lockerbie Bombing Prosecution Hitting Roadblocks at Trial in the Netherlands
2000-07-17.abstract: Prosecution hits roadblocks in trial of two Libyans accused of blowing up Pan Am Flight jetliner over Lockerbie, Scotland, in 1988; some witnesses fail to appear in Netherlands for trial, and testimony of others is less than crystal clear (M)

2000-07-17.title: Money, Jobs, Big Cars: How's an Irishman to Cope?
2000-07-17.abstract: Ireland is in throes of economic boom, growing faster than any other in Western world but struggling to cope with prosperity and its downsides; incomes have risen and jobs are plentiful, but inflation is running at 5.2 percent or three times average of other 10 countries in euro zone; young professionals no longer leave, since there are many job opportunities at home, and those who did a generation ago have returned; disruptive consequence of happy turnaround has been 20 percent-per-year spiral in cost of housing that is pricing middle class out of cities; new prosperity has changed social habits, breaking down traditional pattern of association, but is also source of shame; country is proving to be inhospitable to outsiders, some asylum seekers, arriving in droves and seeking jobs that are going unfilled; maps; photo (M)

2000-07-17.title: THE INNOCENTS/A Special Report; Jailed Unjustly, Peruvians Try to Rebuild Shattered Lives
2000-07-17.abstract: Outside his powder-blue shack, Antonio Alejo waits quietly for customers in need of an oil change or a brake job. He smiles faintly as his youngest child plays with his two stepchildren, running around some burning trash on the sand dune that is his front yard. With business slow, he tends a few geraniums and sunflowers and occasionally fixes a bowl of rice.    Mr. Alejo is slowly picking up the shards of his life.

2000-07-18.title: Sikhs on the Rise in British Colombia
2000-07-18.abstract: Election of Ujjal Dosanjh, Sikh who was born in Punjab, as new premier of British Colombia reflects Canada's rapidly changing ethnic nature, as well as its widespread acceptance of diversity; province's 250,000 Indo-Canadians are increasingly visible as political and economic force and are rapidly entering country's mainstream; most of country's estimated half a million residents of Indian origin are Sikhs; photo; map (M)

2000-07-18.title: Terrorism Suspect Waives Extradition Hearing
2000-07-18.abstract: Mokhtar Haouari, Algerian, waives his right to extradition hearing in Canada and will be turned over to US to face charges of helping Ahmed Ressam, who was arrested in December in Washington State after crossing Canadian border carrying explosives and bomb-making equipment (M)

2000-07-18.title: Putin Visits China In Hope of Strengthening a Strategic Axis
2000-07-18.abstract: Pres Vladimir V Putin of Russia arrives in China on first stop of Asian tour intended to revive his country's faded diplomatic role in region and build what could become Chinese-Russian axis to counter American influence; is expected to sign at least six accords with Chinese leaders, including statement condemning US proposals for missile defense system (M)

2000-07-18.title: Kampala Journal; When a Questionable Past Pays Political Dividends
2000-07-18.abstract: Nasser Ntege Sebaggala, uneducated populist businessman running for president in Uganda, has become local political phenomenon despite the face he served one year in US prison for passing bad checks; will face Pres Yoweri Museveni, who is his opposite in nearly every way, in elections in 2001; photos (M)

2000-07-18.title: Germans Sign Agreement to Pay Forced Laborers of Nazi Era
2000-07-18.abstract: US and Germany approve $5 billion agreement to compensate forced laborers from Nazi era; under agreement German government and German companies will contribute equally to fund to compensate former laborers who were slave workers in concentration camps or German factories; Stuart E Eizenstat, who negotiated on behalf of US, says agreement fills void that has existed for 55 years; historic bargain will compensate Nazi-era victims in more than 20 countries; photo (M)

2000-07-19.title: Thomas Quinn Curtiss Dies, IHT Critic
2000-07-19.abstract: PARIS—  Thomas Quinn Curtiss, who wrote about film and theater for the International Herald Tribune for nearly four decades and contributed to many other publications, including The New York Times and Variety, died Monday in Poissy, outside Paris.

2000-07-19.title: Clinton Delays Trip to Remain at Peace Talks
2000-07-19.abstract: Pres Clinton, seeking to finalize Mideast peace accord, delays his trip to Japan for meeting of leaders of seven leading industrial nations by one day and will stay at Camp David with Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak and Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat; talks, described as increasingly intense and heated, again continue into night; both sides are playing to their constituents at home and preparing public opinion for possible outcomes; photos (M)

2000-07-19.title: Kilmovee Journal; The Gleam in an Irishman's Eye: An Emerald City
2000-07-19.abstract: Proposal by William Thomas to build new city near Kilmovee, in Ireland's isolated west, has spurred national debate and is being embraced by local leaders; comes at time when government planners are working on strategy to spread nation's resources and opportunities more evenly; map; photo (M)

2000-07-19.title: In Long Fight With Israel, Hezbollah Tactics Evolved
2000-07-19.abstract: Hezbollah leader Sheik Nabil Qaouk cites guerrilla group's successful use of television as weapon of modern warfare against Israeli occupation of southern Lebanon, interview; details how group evolved from small band of amateurish gunmen to highly sophisticated tactical operation; cites use of demoralization to help drive Israelis from Lebanon; map; photo (M)

2000-07-19.title: Blair Aims Big Surplus At Crime and Health Issues
2000-07-19.abstract: LONDON—  Britain's chancellor of the Exchequer on Tuesday announced a major increase in public spending over the coming three years, saying the Labour government was determined to use its big budget surplus to finance improvements in health care, education, transportation and crime-fighting.

2000-07-20.title: One More Leaked Memo on Party's Woes Embarrasses Blair
2000-07-20.abstract: British Prime Min Tony Blair's government is embarrassed by leaking to press of memo from government's pollster, Philip Gould, warning New Labor brand has become object of ridicule; note echoed many of concerns raised in secret memo by Blair himself that made its way into newspapers earlier in week (M)

2000-07-20.title: North Korea Reported Open To Halting Missile Program
2000-07-20.abstract: Russian Pres Vladimir V Putin, in announcement intended to remove prime rationale for American missile defense plan, says North Korea will abandon its missile program if other nations will provide it with rockets to launch satellites into space; announcement comes during meeting, Pyongyang, with North Korean leader Kim Jong Il; visit, first by Russian president to North Korea, is part of his intensive round of Asian diplomacy aimed at mobilizing opposition to American anti-missile plan; United States expresses willingness to explore deal in which North Korean satellites would be launched from other nations; photo (M)

2000-07-20.title: A Sudden Turnabout
2000-07-20.abstract: Analysis of refusal of Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak and Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat to let Camp David peace talks end in failure as Pres Clinton prepared to leave; intractable issue of Jerusalem's sovereignty, which Mideast leaders have never dared discuss before, remains threat to talks, but could be deferred in partial accord (M)

2000-07-20.title: Holocaust Group to Step Up Pressure on Austria for Reparations
2000-07-20.abstract: Claims Conference, international organization that seeks compensation for Holocaust survivors, votes at New York City meeting to pressure Austria to admit complicity during Nazi regime and improve reparations to Jews (M)

2000-07-20.title: A Tito Grandchild Battles Nationalism's Excesses
2000-07-20.abstract: Interview with Svetlana Broz on her crusade and book against hardline nationalism that has destroyed Yugoslavia created by her late grandfather, Josip Broz Tito; book recounts stories of gestures of goodness from people who were often on opposing sides in war told to her by patients at hospitals and clinics she served in as doctor after fighting broke out in Bosnia; photos (M)

2000-07-21.title: World Briefing
2000-07-21.abstract: EUROPE    CORSICA: AUTONOMY PLAN ACCEPTED  Corsican nationalists said they had accepted French proposals to grant autonomy to their island in a crucial step toward ending 20 years of separatist violence. Speaking after a key round of talks with officials in Paris, Corsica's politicians said they were satisfied with proposals that opened the door for the island eventually to make its own laws.    (Reuters)

2000-07-21.title: Q & A / Adolf Ogi : Switzerland in No Rush to Join the EU
2000-07-21.abstract: With full United Nations membership on the horizon and a recent vote supporting economic alignment with the European Union, Switzerland seems ready to break with the neutrality of its past. In Bern, Adolf Ogi, the president and defense minister, discussed the new outlook with Robert Kroon of the International Herald Tribune.

2000-07-21.title: Jerusalem Arabs Look Expectantly and Anxiously to Camp David
2000-07-21.abstract: Arabs of East Jerusalem follow reports from Camp David summit with anticipation and anxiety, as well as quiet ambivalence about radical political and economic change if status of Jerusalem is altered; photo; Christian Arabs are alarmed by reports that control of Christian sites is also under discussion; three patriarchs of major Christian churches issue unusual joint protest against any division (M)

2000-07-21.title: Africans Fault Rich Nations On Aid to the World's Poor
2000-07-21.abstract: Leaders of South Africa, Nigeria and Algeria hold news conference in Tokyo to lambast major powers for not acting more swiftly on debt relief and other issues vital to third-world economic growth; photo of Olusegun Obansanjo and Thabo Mbeki; session is held during economic summit meeting; third-world leaders meet with delegations from industrialized nations and with heads of several international corporations (S)

2000-07-21.title: Sun-Drenched Okinawa Is an Uneasy Place to Meet
2000-07-21.abstract: Okinawa, Japan's choice as site of economic summit meeting, is backwater province plagued by tragic past and lacking infrastructure to handle major international gathering; Japanese and American officials fret about potential for mishap and mayhem, given Okinawans' anger at large US military presence; recent arrest of US marine accused of molesting schoolgirl heightens outrage among islanders who have vowed to disrupt summit meeting; about 27,000 people form human chain around Kadena Air Base; photo; map; Pres Clinton is first US president to visit Okinawa since it reverted back to Japan in 1972 (M)

2000-07-22.title: Ivory Coast's Junta Tries To Block Comeback Bid
2000-07-22.abstract: Alassane Ouattara, mild-mannered international banker, vows to win presidential election in Ivory Coast even though constitutional amendment before voters on July 23 is intended to disqualify him; accuses country's military rulers, led by Gen Robert Guei, of trying to hold on to power; photo (M)

2000-07-22.title: Putin Bends Clinton's Ear Hoping to Halt Missile Shield
2000-07-22.abstract: Pres Vladimir V Putin of Russia meets with Pres Clinton during Group of 8 meeting in Okinawa to argue against American deployment of a national missile shield; Clinton makes clear that he has not made a decision, but intends to make a decision during course of summer (M)

2000-07-22.title: The Third Way:Schroeder Soars but Blair Stalls
2000-07-22.abstract: Just over a year ago, a politically floundering Gerhard Schroeder came to London and met Europe's preeminent politician, Tony Blair, to share his aura of invincibility and embrace Mr. Blair's Third Way brand of moderate, center-left politics.

2000-07-22.title: Chipping Away at Embargo Reflects a New Vision of Cuba
2000-07-22.abstract: House and Senate votes to ease restrictions on American travel and certain sales to Cuba are latest evidence of gradual but seismic shift toward normalizing ties between US and Cuba (M)

2000-07-22.title: La Turballe Journal; Oil Washes Away Tourism From Brittany Beaches
2000-07-22.abstract: Tar-like oil from breakup of tanker Erika continues to wash onto beaches at La Turballe and elsewhere in Brittany; towns have been caught in a sort of moral quandary for months about whether to speak up and demand that government do something about pollution or to stay mum and avoid publicizing the problem; only people who have houses in La Turballe or prepaid vacations are returning this summer; some of merchants say their business is off by as much as 50 percent; photo; map (M)

2000-07-23.title: Clinton Hurries To Returnto Camp David
2000-07-23.abstract: Pres Clinton leaves economic summit meeting in Okinawa early to rejoin Mideast peace talks at Camp David between Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak and Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat; photo of signed fax sent by leaders in meeting in Okinawa to Secretary of State Madeleine K Albright, encouraging her efforts at Camp David talks (M)

2000-07-23.title: Ruth Werner, Colorful and Daring Soviet Spy, Dies at 93
2000-07-23.abstract: Ruth Werner, colorful, daring and successful Soviet spy, dies at 93; her life and espionage exploits reviewed; photo (M)

2000-07-23.title: Austria Politician Sometimes Down but Never Out
2000-07-23.abstract: Jorg Haider, whose party's entry into Austrian government was reason 14 other European Union members imposed sanctions on Austria, is technically not in power, but he does not remain in obscurity; on domestic issues, most of his campaign promises have been ignored, but on issue of European Union sanctions, he is very influential; sanctions have done no economic damage, but they humiliate Austrians; Haider has demanded referendum asking Austrians whether they want Austria to use its veto to block all European Union actions until sanctions are lifted; recent poll shows 70 percent favor that move; photo (M)

2000-07-23.title: A Hard Life for Amerasian Children
2000-07-23.abstract: Annette Eddie-Callagain, former United States Air Force lawyer stationed in Okinawa, is waging war on American servicemen who leave after their tour of duty and refuse to pay support for children they fathered with Okinawan women; until recently, there was little that thousands of abandoned Okinawan women and children could do; there is no agreement in force between Japan and United States to make them pay child support; despite lack of bilateral agreement, Eddie-Callagain, now in private practice, is taking her clients' cases directly to child enforcement offices in each of 50 state governments in United States, in hope they will prosecute deadbeat fathers in family court; Amerasian children are treated badly in Japanese public schools, which are notorious for bullying children who do not fit in; photos (M)

2000-07-23.title: John Campbell, 88, an Expert On American Foreign Policy
2000-07-23.abstract: John Coert Campell, former member of State Dept's policy planning council and director of political studies at Council on Foreign Relations, dies at 88 (S)

2000-07-24.title: Deutsche Telekom Deal: Did Barriers Fall in Europe, Just to Rise in the U.S.?
2000-07-24.abstract: Congressional moves to block acquisition of VoiceStream Wireless Corp by Deutsche Telekom, which is owned by German government, could invite retaliation against American communications companies active in Grmany and elsewhere in Europe, and might also run afoul of US commitments to World Trade Organization (M)

2000-07-24.title: Lurid Memoir Reminds Mexicans of Salinas Days
2000-07-24.abstract: Maria Bernal, former mistress of Raul Salinas, imprisoned brother of Mexico's former president, Carlos Salinas de Gortari, writes lurid memoir describing scandalous affair with Salinas that in early 1990's fed public anger with Institutional Revolutionary Party; book, Raul Salinas and I, gives details of sex, graft and abuse of power that are likely to fuel resentments and desire for reform that led Mexicans to vote governing party out; photos (M)

2000-07-24.title: Perils of Summer Travel /Dos and Don'ts for a Happy Holiday : Scams and Land Mines:Avoiding Headaches (and Worse) in Asia
2000-07-24.abstract: CHINA—  Traveling in Asia this summer? Correspondents for the International Herald Tribune and The Washington Post surveyed major tourist destinations and came up with a collection of tips on how best to foil a con artist.

2000-07-24.title: A New Crisis Over Refugees Adrift in Their Own Lands
2000-07-24.abstract: Carolyn McAskie, UN's emergency relief coordinator, is seeking to bring its existing relief agencies into common effort to alleviate plight of millions of internally displaced people, mostly forgotten victims of war who are not regarded as refugees and are struggling to survive without much international help; American UN ambassador, Richard C Holbrooke, is skeptical about so-called collaborative approach and favors designating a new or lead agency to deal with problem (M)

2000-07-24.title: Hope Erodes for Azerbaijan's Sea of Refugees
2000-07-24.abstract: Approximately one million Azerbaijani refugees who fled Nagorno-Karabakh after outbreak of fighting in 1992 between Armenians and Azerbaijanis remain scattered throughout 40 or more camps in Azerbaijan, former Soviet republic whose promised oil riches have yet to materialize; they are insufficiently cared for by their government and on verge of abandonment by international aid organizations; Western diplomats and humanitarian workers say Azerbaijani government is using camps as political pawns in its efforts to negotiate return of Nagorno-Karabakh, which represents 20 percent of its former territory and is occupied by ethnic Aremenians; government reached cease-fire with Armenian forces but no peace agreement that would allow refugees to return home; photos; map (M)

2000-07-25.title: World Briefing
2000-07-25.abstract: EUROPE    YUGOSLAVIA: NEW LAWS AID MILOSEVIC  Yugoslavia's federal Parliament approved new election laws that provide President Slobodan Milosevic with the legal framework to stay in power. The laws define new election procedures under which a president is elected by a majority in a popular vote, regardless of turnout, in a country where the opposition may boycott the polls. They also allow the president a second four-year term. Mr. Milosevic, who was elected in 1997 after serving two terms as president of Serbia, is widely expected to run.   (AP)

2000-07-25.title: Pitkyaranta Journal; She's in the Kitchen, and She's Not Making Blinis
2000-07-25.abstract: Bootlegging is lucrative cottage industry in Pitkyaranta, Russia, where many are jobless and in frantic need of money; map; photo (M)

2000-07-25.title: Battle Heats Up as Airbus and Boeing Showcase Rival Visions
2000-07-25.abstract: FARNBOROUGH, England—  The battle for supremacy between Airbus Industrie and Boeing Co. heated up Monday as both companies announced multibillion-dollar orders in support of their competing visions of 21st century air travel.

2000-07-25.title: Clinton Wades Into the Details of Mideast Negotiations
2000-07-25.abstract: Pres Clinton plunges into nitty-gritty of issue-by-issue deliberations at Camp David summit between Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak and Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat; Israeli and Palestinian officials say negotiators, under Clinton's direct supervision, are engaged in forging working agreement in effort to establish concrete points of accord and highlight gaps; photo (M)

2000-07-25.title: Reporter's Notebook; Weekend Outings for the Inmates of Camp David
2000-07-25.abstract: Reporter's Notebook vignettes of atmosphere at Camp David summit between Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak and Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat (M)

2000-07-26.title: 113 Die in First Crash of a Concorde
2000-07-26.abstract: Air France Concorde carrying mainly German tourists bound for cruise ship in New York crashes in flames shortly after takeoff, killing all 109 passengers and crew and at least 4 people on ground and injuring 5; hits and demolishes small hotel and restaurant, Gonesse, near Paris; crash is first of supersonic Concorde passenger plane; witnesses say one or both of plane's left engines were on fire even as plane sped down runway; Air France president Jean-Cyril Spinetta says cracks were recently found in four of airline's six Concordes, but not on one that crashed; sees no link between engine and cracks; photos (M)

2000-07-26.title: A Symbol of France Crashes in Flames
2000-07-26.abstract: Witnesses say Air France Concorde jet was on fire as it took off from airport near Paris; residents of nearby Gonesse, where plane crashed, are shocked and distressed; maps; drawings table of data on Concorde (M)

2000-07-26.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-07-26.abstract: EUROPE    BRITAIN: NANNY'S PARENTS CLEARED  The parents of Louise Woodward, a former nanny who was convicted in Massachusetts for the second degree murder of a baby in her care, were cleared of trying to defraud an appeal fund set up for their daughter. Judge John Roberts ordered jurors in Chester to find Gary and Susan Woodward, both 44, not guilty of defrauding the fund of more than $13,500, saying the prosecution failed to prove some of the money in the fund was not donated solely for the parents' use. The conviction of Louise Woodward, now 22, was later downgraded to manslaughter and she was freed after serving 279 days in jail.   (AP)

2000-07-26.title: Arafat's Allies Say He Stands Taller at Home for Firmness at Talks
2000-07-26.abstract: Palestinian officials say they will presss ahead with plans to declare independent state on Sept 13 in wake of failure of Camp David talks to produce accord; hold Yasir Arafat made all concessions possible from Palestinian side before talks; say firm stance he apparently took at talks will boost his standing among Palestinians; blame Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak for impasse; photo (M)

2000-07-26.title: A Long Long Trail Is Awinding, Right Across Canada
2000-07-26.abstract: Canada prepares to open Trans Canada Trail, new 10,000-mile network that will link Atlantic, Pacific and Arctic Oceans; building of trail, billed as world's longest recreational trail, has become exercise in nation building as thousands have volunteered for relay teams snaking way across nation with water from three oceans for Sept inauguration, near Ottawa; 250,000 Canadians have donated money for trail; map (M)

2000-07-27.title: Hero's Welcome for Arafat, From Those Who Showed Up
2000-07-27.abstract: Palestinians give Yasir Arafat hero's welcome for holding fast at Camp David to demands for sovereignty over all of East Jerusalem, Gaza; frenzied welcome seems carefully orchestrated by Arafat's Fatah movement; Ramallah rally in support of Arafat attracts only 200 people; many Palestians seem deeply disllusioned with him for failing to improve living conditions; photo (M)

2000-07-27.title: Barak Wins One Thing: The President's Praise
2000-07-27.abstract: Pres Clinton's praise for Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak at end of failed Camp David peace talks means Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat may get less international sympathy and financial aid than he hoped if he declares Palestinian state unilaterally in Sept; Barak has been working to tilt world opinion back toward Israel since he took office year ago; his aides say he believes he has made Israel seem more reasonable party in peace effort and perhaps even bolstered Israel's ties with United States; Arafat seen having won needed domestic support (M)

2000-07-27.title: Clinton Says Peace Needs More Time Now
2000-07-27.abstract: Pres Clinton says Palestinians and Israelis need more time for internal debate and reflection before reaching peace accord; administration officials say American envoy Dennis B Ross will visit region in coming weeks to gauge any post-summit progress; rule out follow-up talks in United States any time soon; describe Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak as far more willing to deal than Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat (M)

2000-07-27.title: Final Approval on Swiss Holocaust Claims
2000-07-27.abstract: Federal Judge Edward R Korman gives final approval to $1.25 billion accord to settle suit by Holocaust survivors against Swiss banks that allegedly hoarded assets deposited in World War II (M)

2000-07-27.title: For Executives With Money, a Time-Saver
2000-07-27.abstract: Passengers who fly on Concorde, commenting in wake of Air France crash, say time saved using supersonic jet to cross Atlantic is well worth cost and relative discomfort; express few qualms about flying on jet again (M)

2000-07-28.title: French Account of Concorde Crash
2000-07-28.abstract: Excerpts from communique issued by French government about crash of Air France Concorde, translated by The New York Times (M)

2000-07-28.title: Luba Journal; A Corner of Africa Where Dreams Gush Like Oil
2000-07-28.abstract: Equitorial Guinea, tiny and once penniless country on Africa's west coast in region awash with oil, has finally hit the jackpot, with perhaps 120,000 barrels of crude oil a day; Pres Teodor Obiang Nguema Mbasogo, in novel strategy designed to use new wealth to spur development in all parts of country, is moving entire government from capital of Malabo to city of Bata, and will keep moving it from town to town before returning to Malabo; scene in town of Luba, which is to be developed as port for oil trade, described; map; photo (Luba Journal) (M)

2000-07-28.title: Kazakhstan Is Suspected Of Oil Bribes In the Millions
2000-07-28.abstract: Swiss court lifts order freezing bank accounts holding roughly $100 million that authorities suspect Western oil companies used to bribe senior Kazakh officials; court does not open accounts to Kazakhstan because they remain subject to parallel freeze by United States; banks involved include Credit Agricole Indosuez and Banque Pictet; James H Giffen and his Mercator Corp in New York are being investigated on suspicion of improperly funneling money from Philips Petroleum Co, Exxon Mobil and BP Amoco (M)

2000-07-28.title: Playing for (Political) Time on China Trade
2000-07-28.abstract: Senate, under strong pressure from Clinton administration to act on stalled China trade bill, votes to open debate, overcoming procedural hurdle to schedule full consideration of measure in early September; bill, which was passed by House with sense of urgency in May, has become hostage to other legislative goals, efforts to punish China for weapons proliferation, political maneuvering and general gridlock (M)

2000-07-28.title: An Eagerness For New Talks In the Mideast
2000-07-28.abstract: Prim Min Ehud Barak and Yasir Arafat make it clear that they are eager to put failed Camp David talks behind them and move forward with renewed peace effort; two sides confirm that negotiators will meet on July 30 at border of Gaza Strip, and say that ideas floated at Camp David have carried peace effort further than ever before; two leaders suggest that some form of agreement, perhaps only partial and possibly excluding deal-breaking issue of Jerusalem, may yet be possible in weeks and months ahead; photo (M)

2000-07-29.title: Arctic Shortcut for Shipping Raises New Fears in Canada
2000-07-29.abstract: Opening of Arctic to commercial navigation, result of global warming, could bring biggest change in American shipping routes since Panama Canal opened in 1914, situation that concerns environmentalists and native Americans who rely on polar sea for basic items; over all, late summer icecap of North Pole has shrunk 6 percent over last 20 years, and average thickness of Arctic icecap has thinned 42 percent since 1950's; some scientists say that if these trends continue, polar icecap, which covers 80 percent of Arctic Ocean at its September minimum, could vanish every summer within next 50 years; even now, shipping lines are finalizing plans to send freighters with reinforced hulls steaming in summer months through Northeast Passage; photos; map (M)

2000-07-29.title: World Briefing
2000-07-29.abstract: EUROPE    NORTHERN IRELAND: GUERRILLAS FREED  Eighty-six hardened guerrilla fighters walked free from prison in the last wave of 428 early releases of convicted bombers and killers mandated by the Northern Ireland peace accord. The Maze prison, home to 1,800 inmates during the years of sectarian violence that claimed more than 3,600 lives, was left with only 15 prisoners and is to be closed by the end of the year.   Warren Hoge (NYT)

2000-07-29.title: Ransom Money Buys Guns, Gold and Jade : Filipino Rebels Discover Kidnapping Is Lucrative
2000-07-29.abstract: JOLO, Philippines—  Jewelers are trekking into the jungle surrounding this once-flourishing commercial center of the southern Philippines in search of new customers for gold and jade. Gun dealers are offering American and Chinese rifles and pistols. Drug dealers provide shabu, an amphetamine called the "poor man's cocaine," produced in makeshift local laboratories or smuggled from China.

2000-07-29.title: Germans Say Nine Wounded By Bomb Were Immigrants
2000-07-29.abstract: German police and prosecutors say 9 people wounded by bomb in Dusseldorf are immigrants, 6 of them Jews; say victims had just left their daily German class when bomb exploded; Interior Min Otto Schily says it is reasonable to assume that attack had racist motive; number of Jews in Germany has been growing rapidly over last decade; bombing against them would be blow to Germany's international image and worrying indication that extreme right is becoming bolder and more organized (M)

2000-07-29.title: In the President's Words: U.S. Commitment to Israel
2000-07-29.abstract: Excerpts from Israeli television interview with Pres Clinton (M)

2000-07-30.title: At Conference, Billy Graham's Torch Moves South
Bosnians, Ending Exile, Cope With Old Hatreds
Barak Asked Clinton To Raise Embassy Issue
2000-07-30.abstract: Clinton administration officials say Pres Clinton, in raising prospect of moving American Embassy in Israel to Jerusalem, responded to unusual request made by Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak as he left failed Camp David peace talks; national security adviser Samuel R Berger says Clinton decided to use interview on Israeli television to help Barak shore up his political position in Israel; Barak reportedly also asked number of congressmen to reintroduce resolution on moving embassy (M)

2000-07-30.title: Drug Runners Are Finding the Going Easy in Haiti
2000-07-30.abstract: Colombian cocaine traffickers, who smuggle drugs through impoverished Haiti to Puerto Rico, United States and neighboring Dominican Republic, exploit nation's inexperienced, understaffed and underpaid police and courts; mobs that gather when drug planes land in hope of snatching few bags of cocaine have become perhaps Haiti's only front-line deterrent to smugglers; smugglers bring cash, crime and corruption; Haiti's political paralysis hampers efforts of American anti-drug agents to focus more on nation; map; photo (M)

2000-07-30.title: Arafat, on Diplomatic Tour, Accuses Israelis of 'Big Lies'
2000-07-30.abstract: Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat begins diplomatic tour aimed at solidifying international support in wake of breakup of Camp David peace talks; will meet with French Pres Jacques Chirac before visits to Russia and key Arab nations; calls Israeli accounts of talks 'big lies;' Palestinians, despite growing anger and frustration, say they will resume talks with Israelis; photo (M)

2000-07-31.title: Camp David Terms Roil West Bank Settlers
2000-07-31.abstract: Many Jewish settlers on West Bank are roiled by outcome of Camp David summit, even though talks faltered; approximately 40,000 settlers, especially those around hot spots of Hebron and vicinity, will be left under Palestinian rule in any peace agreement; many must choose either to remain or accept whatever compensation is offered to return inside sovereign borders of Israel; feel government, which once regarded them as pioneers, is now rejecting them as liabilities; maps; photos (M)

2000-07-31.title: Barak Seems in Position to Survive Vote in Parliament
2000-07-31.abstract: Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak, after complex backroom maneuvering on eve of no-confidence vote in parliament, seems likely to survive vote and keep government afloat for next three months; much speculation centered on whether Foreign Min David Levy, who has history of changing sides, would resign over Camp David peace talks and return to roots in Likud Party causing voting shift to opposition in Parliament and forcing new elections; Levy says he will remain in cabinet, and will abstain from no-confidence vote; says he will try to persuade Barak to create broad-based government of national unity; senior Israeli and Palestinian officials meet, indicating effort to find peace is still alive despite failed summit (M)

2000-07-31.title: Rebel Cease-Fire May Signal an Easing of Tension in Kashmir
2000-07-31.abstract: Tensions in Kashmir may be lessening for first time since fighting flared in summer 1999; rebel group Hizbul Mujahedeen has announced unilateral three-month cease-fire, India has invited leaders of all militant groups for talks and Indian Army announces suspension of military operations against Mujahedeedn, though not against other militants; fragility of peace initiative is underscored by threat from Hizbul to undo it unless India agrees to make talks unconditional; map (M)

2000-07-31.title: L'Anse aux Meadows Journal; Newfoundland Happily Rediscovers the Vikings
2000-07-31.abstract: 1,000th anniversary of Leif Ericson's landing on L'Anse Aux Meadows, establishing short-lived Viking colony on Newfoundland's northernmost tip, is re-enacted by group of Icelandic men, who sailed from Greenland aboard replica of old Viking vessel; commemmoration is part of Newfoundland's efforts to shake off label as Canada's poorest province; long isolated from rest of North America, it is well on way to meeting self-imposed goal of attracting one tourist each year for each of 540,000 residents; map; photos (M)

2000-07-31.title: Boeing Backs Start-Up's Project : In-Flight Shower:Ultimate Perk?
2000-07-31.abstract: FARNBOROUGH, England—  For globe-trotting executives who expect to be able to work and relax at 35,000 feet as easily as if they were on the ground, an in-flight shower may be the ultimate accessory.
